title: Peter Wolf left one Boston art school to form the J. Geils Band. Six decades later, he earned an honorary doctorate from another.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Peter Wolf, known for the J. Geils Band, received an honorary doctorate from Massachusetts College of Art and Design.
Wolf's journey included college-hopping as a pseudo-art student before he pursued music, eventually forming his famous band.
Wolf is set to release an album and may work on new books following his honorary degree achievement.

title: Boston 25 hires Lauren Walsh for newly-created role
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Lauren Walsh hired by Boston 25 as a sports media journalist, a new role for the station.
Walsh, a Medford native, previously worked as a sports anchor in Nashville and interned at NESN.
The Syracuse graduate has also worked in Burlington, Vt. and Winston-Salem, N.C. before Nashville.

title: Somerville restaurant owner says viral ‘pool water’ reply to customer review was unauthorized
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Olivia's Kitchen in Somerville faced criticism after a sharp online response to a customer review was shared on Reddit.
Owner Simona Sbano stated the response was unauthorized and the responsible employee was let go after other incidents.
The restaurant will now serve tap water upon request, addressing concerns about its previous policy on water service.

title: With split vote, City Council effort to reject Wu’s budget fails
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston City Council split 6-6 on rejecting Mayor Wu's budget proposal, with Councilor Gabriela Coletta Zapata absent.
Mayor Wu refused to increase spending despite pressures, emphasizing a fiscally responsible approach during budget discussions.
Boston faces budget deficits due to unforeseen costs like historic snowfall, with requests to use reserve funds for adjustments.

title: Kevin McHale questions Celtics’ 3-point heavy approach: ‘The ball has got to touch the paint’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kevin McHale critiques Celtics' reliance on 3-point shooting, suggesting more offensive diversity is needed.
McHale emphasizes importance of attacking the paint and generating free-throw opportunities for the Celtics.
Celtics struggled with poor 3-point shooting and lost 3-1 series lead to the 76ers in recent playoffs.

title: Mass. pols issue report on possible effects of Haiti TPS termination on local health care
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts politicians report that ending Haitian TPS could worsen healthcare workforce shortages and affect patient care quality.
The report estimates that about 2,000 direct care workers in Massachusetts are at risk of losing work authorization.
Senators Warren, Markey, and Representative Pressley highlight that the decision could disproportionately impact Massachusetts' significant Haitian community.

title: This New England town hosts one of America’s best 4th of July celebrations, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Bristol, Rhode Island hosts the oldest continuous Fourth of July celebration, starting in 1785.
USA Today ranked Bristol's Fourth of July Celebration as the 5th best in the nation for 2026.
The three-week event in Bristol includes concerts, parades, and attracts over 200,000 spectators.

title: England use a Beatles soundtrack to reveal controversial World Cup squad picks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: England's World Cup squad announcement used a Beatles-themed video, featuring 'Come Together' and American-themed visuals.
High-profile players like Trent Alexander-Arnold and Phil Foden were left out of Thomas Tuchel's 26-man squad.
Surprise selections include Ivan Toney and Djed Spence, despite recent limited play and injury, respectively.

title: Stephen Colbert bids farewell to ‘Late Show’ with star-studded finale
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert hosted the final 'Late Show' episode, featuring Paul McCartney as the last guest at Ed Sullivan Theater.
The finale included celebrity cameos like Paul Rudd and Bryan Cranston, and a musical performance by Elvis Costello.
Colbert's farewell included surreal elements, like a wormhole and a shrinking snow globe, referencing 'St. Elsewhere.'

title: Head to these fan favorite beach towns this Memorial Day weekend
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ogunquit, Maine was voted New England's best beach town in Boston.com's Battle of the Beach Towns.
JetBlue will discontinue service from Manchester-Boston Regional Airport starting July 8.
Harwich was chosen as the best Cape Cod town by over 850 Boston.com readers.
